## HPE Perspective

HPE's AI-at-the-Edge strategy targets the growing demand for real-time inference at the point of data creation. Today, most enterprise AI workloads run in centralized cloud or on-prem data centers, creating latency and bandwidth bottlenecks for time-sensitive applications like visual inspection, predictive maintenance, and autonomous logistics. HPE ProLiant edge servers and GreenLake for Edge already serve a subset of early adopters, but the current sales motion is hardware-first and project-based, leaving recurring revenue on the table.

Competitive pressure: Hyperscalers (AWS Outposts, Azure Stack HCI) are bundling inference runtimes with their edge appliances, and NVIDIA is expanding its Jetson ecosystem into enterprise-grade deployments. If HPE does not offer a managed, model-lifecycle service at the edge, customers will default to hyperscaler ecosystems and HPE risks being reduced to a commodity hardware supplier.

Strategic opportunity: A GreenLake-based Edge AI Inference-as-a-Service (EIaaS) offering would create sticky recurring revenue, increase GreenLake attach rates for edge deployments, and position HPE as the neutral infrastructure partner for multi-model, multi-framework edge AI.

## Customer Perspective

### Manufacturing Customers (Discrete & Process)

"We run YOLO-based visual inspection on three production lines. The models were trained in Azure, but inference must happen on-prem within 50ms. Right now we cobbled together an NVIDIA Jetson cluster ourselves, but we have no way to update models without sending an engineer on-site. Downtime during model updates costs us $12K per hour."

"Our OT team doesn't trust cloud connectivity for safety-critical defect detection. They need air-gapped or intermittent-connectivity support. HPE hardware is already in our factories—if you could manage the AI runtime on top, we'd consolidate vendors."

"We evaluated AWS Panorama, but it locks us into SageMaker for training. We use PyTorch internally and need framework flexibility."

### Retail & Logistics Customers

"We need real-time shelf analytics across 200 stores. Each store has a small server closet, no IT staff. Model updates need to happen over-the-air without store disruption. Our current vendor charges per-camera licensing, which doesn't scale."

"Loss prevention AI is a board-level priority. We piloted a startup solution, but they can't meet our data residency requirements in the EU. HPE's sovereign edge positioning is attractive."

### Healthcare Customers

"Radiology AI inference needs to run inside hospital networks due to HIPAA and patient data sovereignty. Latency to cloud is unacceptable for real-time surgical guidance. We need an on-prem inference platform that our biomedical engineers can manage without deep ML expertise."

"We have 14 different AI models from 5 vendors running on separate appliances. Consolidating onto a single managed platform would reduce our support burden by 60%."

## Root Causes & Barriers

1. No unified MLOps pipeline from cloud training to edge deployment—customers build bespoke bridges.
2. Edge sites lack IT staff; model lifecycle management (versioning, rollback, A/B testing) is manual.
3. Security and compliance requirements (air-gap, data sovereignty) are not addressed by hyperscaler edge offerings.
4. HPE's current edge portfolio is hardware-centric; no differentiated software/service layer for AI workloads.
5. Internal HPE: Edge sales teams lack AI competency; channel partners are not enabled on inference workloads.

## Impact

Failure to act within 12 months risks ceding the edge AI inference market to hyperscalers and NVIDIA.
90% of enterprise edge AI projects stall at pilot stage due to operational complexity—HPE can capture this gap.
TAM for edge AI inference services estimated at $8.2B by 2027, growing 34% CAGR.
Customer interviews indicate willingness to pay 2-3x hardware-only margins for a managed inference service with guaranteed SLAs.

## Expected Outcomes

Launch EIaaS on GreenLake within 6 months for manufacturing vertical, expand to retail and healthcare within 12 months.
Achieve $50M ARR in Year 1 from conversion of existing edge hardware customers.
Reduce customer time-to-inference from weeks to hours via pre-validated model deployment blueprints.
Increase GreenLake edge attach rate from 12% to 40%.
Establish HPE as the vendor-neutral alternative to hyperscaler lock-in for edge AI.

## Assumptions About Customer Segments

### Manufacturing (Primary Segment)
- 300+ existing HPE ProLiant edge customers in discrete manufacturing
- 70% have active AI/ML initiatives but struggle with edge deployment
- Willing to pay $2K-$5K/month per site for managed inference
- Decision maker: VP Manufacturing Operations + CTO jointly

### Retail & Logistics (Secondary Segment)
- High-volume, low-touch deployment model needed (200+ sites)
- Per-camera or per-model pricing preferred over per-server
- 18-month sales cycle due to procurement complexity

### Healthcare (Tertiary Segment)
- Highest compliance requirements but highest willingness to pay
- Regulatory approval timelines extend deployment by 6-12 months
- Strong reference-ability value for other regulated industries
